items.claim: Conor McGregor suffered a knee injury, likely a torn ACL or MCL, during his fight against Max Holloway.
items.quote: he actually had a knee injury. I think he tore his ACL or MCL midfight.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's power has not translated well to the upper weight classes.
items.quote: I know his power hasn't translated too much in these upper weight classes

items.claim: Conor McGregor's counter-striking and eyes are among the best in MMA history.
items.quote: his eyes and counter strikes are elite. You know, they're some of the best in the entire game that we've ever seen in MMA.

items.claim: Max Holloway has been getting tagged and hurt more frequently in his recent fights.
items.quote: Max has been getting tagged a lot more lately, right? ... he's been getting hurt a lot more lately.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's puncher's chance is limited specifically to the first round.
items.quote: not only does he have a puncher's chance, he only has it in round one, in my opinion.

items.claim: Max Holloway possesses a significant cardio and conditioning advantage over Conor McGregor.
items.quote: He has way too much five round experience, way too much of a cardio/cond conditioning advantage to lose in the later rounds here.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is currently carrying more muscle mass than at any previous point in his career.
items.quote: this is the biggest he's ever been and he's carrying around more muscle than he's ever had.

items.claim: Conor McGregor used performance-enhancing drugs to recover from a leg injury.
items.quote: he's openly admitted to being on steroids because he said in an Ariel Hwani interview that I watched that he had to take them. ... I had to get on all of this stuff because they were telling me that I could not walk again.

items.claim: Max Holloway has a superior ability to recover from bad positions and adversity compared to Conor McGregor.
items.quote: I genuinely feel like it's going to be so hard for Connor to finish Max, but not that hard for him to maybe wobble him ... I trust Max to come back from that.
